headlineSummary: OpenAI drew the most negative sentiment on limited evidence, Google Gemini was also negative, and Anthropic was mixed after praise for productivity was offset by business-model doubts [E2][E3][E1].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was viewed negatively on limited evidence, with one comment saying ChatGPT’s answer was unrelated and wrong and another doubting the company’s business model [E2][E3].
Google Gemini came across negatively on limited evidence, with a commenter saying it mostly gets in the way rather than help [E2].
Anthropic was mixed: one user praised Claude as a strong productivity multiplier, while another questioned whether its business model can hold up [E1][E3].

evidence.excerpt: I've been using Claude to work on a medium-sized (100+kLoc) codebase, and it's a great productivity multiplier. Putting hours into creating a good AGENTS file is more improved results a lot. I find that over time it picks up the codebase quite well. Tedious tasks that would take a day are now a matter of a few prompts.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly praises Claude as a 'great productivity multiplier' and says tasks that used to take a day now take only a few prompts.

evidence.excerpt: He sent me a ChatGPT screenshot with the answer. I replied that it had nothing to do with my question and everything there was wrong. A minute later he sent me another ChatGPT screenshot. He didn’t even read the AI’s answer. That's just rude and borderline psychotic behavior. It's still a bit better at my workplace but irritating nonetheless - my boss would "research" a feature and prep notes in our wiki with some gemini chatbot exchanges attached. This is a of course no specification, but it's supposed to be a good base point to start working on the feature. Gemini already chose the coding libraries and concepts, so to the outsider it just seems like all that's needed is to code that into the product. Of course, it's not that simple and it mostly gets in the way rather than help.

annotations.rationale: The excerpt explicitly names ChatGPT and says its answer 'had nothing to do with my question' and that 'everything there was wrong,' which is direct criticism of output quality.
The commenter explicitly names Gemini and says it 'mostly gets in the way rather than help,' a clear negative judgment about its usefulness for the task.

evidence.excerpt: So how do openai and anthropic plan to keep customers when GLM-5.1 is just as good and open source and a lot cheaper? I don't see the business model working.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly questions OpenAI's ability to retain customers against a cheaper competitor and says 'I don't see the business model working.'
The commenter explicitly questions Anthropic's ability to keep customers versus a cheaper alternative and says the business model does not work.
